sentences.quote: Recent classical tensor-network methods --- belief propagation on 2D/3D PEPS networks (Tindall, Mello, Fishman, Stoudenmire, Sels, \textit{Science} \textbf{392}, 868 (2026)) and Chebyshev-TN density-matrix expansions on quasicrystal Hamiltonians (Antão, Sun, Fumega, Lado, \textit{PRL} \textbf{136}, 156601 (2026)) --- have collectively narrowed the gap between classical and quantum many-body simulation.
sentences.quote_normalized: recent classical tensor-network methods --- belief propagation on 2d/3d peps networks (tindall, mello, fishman, stoudenmire, sels, science 392, 868 (2026)) and chebyshev-tn density-matrix expansions on quasicrystal hamiltonians (antão, sun, fumega, lado, prl 136, 156601 (2026)) --- have collectively narrowed the gap between classical and quantum many-body simulation.
sentences.agent_notes: Both inline-cited Tindall-Sels Science 392/868 (2026) and Antão et al. PRL 136/156601 (2026) resolve in CITATION_REGISTRY (citations.py L10314, L10296).

sentences.quote: We formalize a joint substrate in the Lean~4 proof assistant and ship a kernel-verified \textit{simulability demarcation biconditional}: a quantum many-body system on a factor graph $G$ with factor weights $f_a$ and categorical-Chern data $(c_0, c_1)$ is classically simulable in the combined BP / Chebyshev-TN regime if and only if (i) a structural tree indicator on the factor graph is below a fixed threshold (the underlying topology is tree-like and factor weights are non-negative) AND (ii) the topological coefficient $c_1$ vanishes.
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize a joint substrate in the lean~4 proof assistant and ship a kernel-verified simulability demarcation biconditional: a quantum many-body system on a factor graph $g$ with factor weights $f_a$ and categorical-chern data $(c_0, c_1)$ is classically simulable in the combined bp / chebyshev-tn regime if and only if (i) a structural tree indicator on the factor graph is below a fixed threshold (the underlying topology is tree-like and factor weights are non-negative) and (ii) the topological coefficient $c_1$ vanishes.
sentences.agent_notes: Headline biconditional verified at AnalogHawkingDemarcation.lean. Note the remediation: terminology softened from 'demarcation theorem' (more controversial first-pass claim) to 'simulability demarcation biconditional'; footnote in tex L28-34 now scopes the priority claim to a 'best of our knowledge after scoped search' hedge.

sentences.quote: To the best of our knowledge after a scoped search of GitHub topics \texttt{lean4} / \texttt{coq} / \texttt{agda}, the Mathlib / mathlib4 indexes, and the Coq UniMath / Agda categories ecosystems (2026-05-26), no prior proof-assistant formalization of a classical-simulability vs.\ quantum-advantage demarcation on tensor-network factor graphs exists.
sentences.quote_normalized: to the best of our knowledge after a scoped search of github topics lean4 / coq / agda, the mathlib / mathlib4 indexes, and the coq unimath / agda categories ecosystems (2026-05-26), no prior proof-assistant formalization of a classical-simulability vs. quantum-advantage demarcation on tensor-network factor graphs exists.
sentences.agent_notes: Hedged priority claim added in remediation footnote. The hedging (scoped enumeration of registries searched + 'we welcome notification of prior work') discharges the Stage-13 hedging-discipline concern. Not falsifiable by structural check; appropriately scoped.

sentences.quote: Outside this regime --- loopy factor-graph topology OR non-trivial Chern coefficient --- quantum processors retain a structural simulation advantage at the level of the formalized substrate.
sentences.quote_normalized: outside this regime --- loopy factor-graph topology or non-trivial chern coefficient --- quantum processors retain a structural simulation advantage at the level of the formalized substrate.
sentences.agent_notes: Contrapositive verified at AnalogHawkingDemarcation.lean. Remediation hedged from 'genuine simulation advantage' to 'structural simulation advantage at the level of the formalized substrate' — appropriately scoped to substrate vs general physics.

sentences.quote: The Lean substrate is fully generic over the factor-graph type parameters; analog-Hawking applications via the Kibble-Zurek-Unruh correspondence are sketched at the cross-module substrate level in Section~\ref{sec:kzu-application}, with explicit per-platform (BEC, polariton, graphene Dirac fluid) instantiation bridges deferred to the D-bundle absorption pass.
sentences.quote_normalized: the lean substrate is fully generic over the factor-graph type parameters; analog-hawking applications via the kibble-zurek-unruh correspondence are sketched at the cross-module substrate level in section~, with explicit per-platform (bec, polariton, graphene dirac fluid) instantiation bridges deferred to the d-bundle absorption pass.

Honest scoping matches the Lean source (AnalogHawkingDemarcation does not import KibbleZurekUnruh).

sentences.quote: The 65 substantive theorems shipped across seven contributing modules have kernel-only axiom closure $[\mathtt{propext}, \mathtt{Classical.choice}, \mathtt{Quot.sound}]$ with zero project-local axioms; the structural tree-indicator predicate uses \texttt{Classical.byCases} on an undecidable graph property and so the ``threshold check'' is information-theoretic, not algorithmic, at this substrate layer.
sentences.quote_normalized: the 65 substantive theorems shipped across seven contributing modules have kernel-only axiom closure $[\mathtt{propext}, \mathtt{classical.choice}, \mathtt{quot.sound}]$ with zero project-local axioms; the structural tree-indicator predicate uses classical.bycases on an undecidable graph property and so the "threshold check" is information-theoretic, not algorithmic, at this substrate layer.

Matches paper claim. Zero project-local axioms verified: 2 grep hits on '^axiom ' in BHEntropyMicroscopic.lean / LaplaceMethod.lean are docstring text ("axiom by making...", "axiom asserted..."), not declarations. Information-theoretic-not-algorithmic hedge correctly added on the Classical.byCases use — closes the prior 'computable threshold' overclaim.

sentences.quote: The boundary between classical and quantum many-body simulation has moved substantially over 2025--2026.
sentences.quote_normalized: the boundary between classical and quantum many-body simulation has moved substantially over 2025--2026.

sentences.quote: Tindall, Mello, Fishman, Stoudenmire, and Sels demonstrated that classical tensor-network contraction via belief propagation matches the D-Wave Advantage2 quantum annealer on 300+ qubit spin-glass dynamics across 2D square, 3D cubic, and 3D diamond lattices~\cite{TindallMelloFishmanStoudenmireSels2026Science392}.
sentences.quote_normalized: tindall, mello, fishman, stoudenmire, and sels demonstrated that classical tensor-network contraction via belief propagation matches the d-wave advantage2 quantum annealer on 300+ qubit spin-glass dynamics across 2d square, 3d cubic, and 3d diamond lattices~.
sentences.agent_notes: Tindall et al. Science 2026 in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: Antão, Sun, Fumega, and Lado complemented this with a Chebyshev-TN density-matrix algorithm that computes local Chern markers on quasicrystal Hamiltonians with hundreds of millions of sites~\cite{AntaoSunFumegaLado2026PRL136}.
sentences.quote_normalized: antão, sun, fumega, and lado complemented this with a chebyshev-tn density-matrix algorithm that computes local chern markers on quasicrystal hamiltonians with hundreds of millions of sites~.

sentences.quote: Together these two results bracket a substantial swath of quantum-simulation territory in which classical methods are competitive.

sentences.quote: Where exactly does the boundary lie? When does classical simulation suffice, and when do quantum processors retain a structural advantage?

sentences.quote: This paper formalizes a substantive demarcation criterion at the Lean~4 substrate level, producing a kernel-verified biconditional that decomposes the simulability question into two load-bearing axes:
sentences.quote_normalized: this paper formalizes a substantive demarcation criterion at the lean~4 substrate level, producing a kernel-verified biconditional that decomposes the simulability question into two load-bearing axes:
sentences.agent_notes: Paper-self-description.

sentences.quote: \textit{Tree-indicator condition}: a structural indicator on the factor graph (encoding ``this graph is a tree'' at the predicate level) must be at or below the fixed threshold $1/2$ (equivalently, the factor graph is tree-like and the factor weights are non-negative). The Lean implementation $\mathtt{loopCorrectionRate}\,G := \mathtt{if}\ \mathrm{IsTreeFactorGraph}\,G\ \mathtt{then}\ 0\ \mathtt{else}\ 1$ is a $\{0,1\}$-valued indicator, not a continuous Cramér-type large-deviation rate function.
sentences.quote_normalized: tree-indicator condition: a structural indicator on the factor graph (encoding "this graph is a tree" at the predicate level) must be at or below the fixed threshold $1/2$ (equivalently, the factor graph is tree-like and the factor weights are non-negative). the lean implementation $loopcorrectionrate\,g := if\ istreefactorgraph\,g\ then\ 0\ else\ 1$ is a $\{0,1\}$-valued indicator, not a continuous cramér-type large-deviation rate function.
sentences.agent_notes: Substantive remediation: the round-1 'LDP rate function condition' framing has been honestly downgraded to 'Tree-indicator condition' with the $\{0,1\}$-valued indicator made explicit, and the LDP-Cramér gap disclosed as a separate ~200-400 LoC deliverable. This is exactly the discipline the round-1 reviewer was asking for. Substantive equivalence still holds via the two cited theorems.

sentences.quote: \textit{Topological condition}: the categorical Chern coefficient $c_1$ of the system must vanish.

sentences.quote: A Lean~4 formalization of belief propagation on bipartite factor graphs in the Yedidia-Freeman-Weiss 2003 convention, with 24 substantive theorems covering message-update positivity, factorization, fixed-point stability, and Bethe free-energy substrate (Section~\ref{sec:bp-substrate}).
sentences.quote_normalized: a lean~4 formalization of belief propagation on bipartite factor graphs in the yedidia-freeman-weiss 2003 convention, with 24 substantive theorems covering message-update positivity, factorization, fixed-point stability, and bethe free-energy substrate (section~).

sentences.quote: A kernel-verified biconditional characterization of BP-favorable factor graphs in terms of a tree-indicator threshold: $\mathrm{IsBPConvergenceFavorable}\ G\ f_a \iff \mathrm{loopCorrectionRate}\ G \leq 1/2 \land (\forall a\, y,\, 0 \leq f_a(y))$ where $\mathrm{loopCorrectionRate}$ is a $\{0,1\}$-valued structural tree indicator (not a continuous LDP rate function in the present substrate; see Section~\ref{sec:ldp-bp}).
sentences.quote_normalized: a kernel-verified biconditional characterization of bp-favorable factor graphs in terms of a tree-indicator threshold: $isbpconvergencefavorable\ g\ f_a \iff loopcorrectionrate\ g \leq 1/2 \land (\forall a\, y,\, 0 \leq f_a(y))$ where $loopcorrectionrate$ is a $\{0,1\}$-valued structural tree indicator (not a continuous ldp rate function in the present substrate; see section~).
sentences.agent_notes: Headline biconditional verified. Substantive remediation: contribution honestly describes the loopCorrectionRate as a $\{0,1\}$-valued structural tree indicator, NOT a continuous LDP rate function. The scope-cap is disclosed in-line.

sentences.quote: A categorical-Chern $\leftrightarrow$ real-space-Chern bridge formalized via the Chebyshev tensor-network expansion, with substantively distinct crystalline (band-edge $x = 1$) and quasicrystalline (band-edge $x = -1$) limits whose difference $2 c_1$ extracts the topological invariant (Section~\ref{sec:chern-bridge}).
sentences.quote_normalized: a categorical-chern $\leftrightarrow$ real-space-chern bridge formalized via the chebyshev tensor-network expansion, with substantively distinct crystalline (band-edge $x = 1$) and quasicrystalline (band-edge $x = -1$) limits whose difference $2 c_1$ extracts the topological invariant (section~).
sentences.agent_notes: All three named theorems exist in ChernBridge.lean.

sentences.quote: A unified demarcation theorem (Section~\ref{sec:demarcation}) combining the LDP-BP and Chern contributions: a system is classically simulable iff BP-LDP-favorable AND $c_1 = 0$. Contrapositive yields the quantum-advantage regime explicitly.
sentences.quote_normalized: a unified demarcation theorem (section~) combining the ldp-bp and chern contributions: a system is classically simulable iff bp-ldp-favorable and $c_1 = 0$. contrapositive yields the quantum-advantage regime explicitly.
